title: External Integrations, Distributed Effects, and Resilience · Claim Audit: Distributed Effect
summary: The product relies on APIs, queues, models, identity, payments, files, or notifications whose delays, duplication, outages, drift, and policy changes are hidden. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone distributed effect increment in controlled integration and resilience evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.
Wrap external behavior behind owned contracts and test requests, responses, errors, limits, version drift, and semantic mismatch with controlled fixtures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Distributed Effect · Theory Distributed Effect
steps.instruction:
theory case — A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Distributed Effect contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Distributed Effect · Theory Distributed Effect.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules. For Predict Distributed Effect · Theory Distributed Effect, keep Distributed Effect connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Distributed Effect condition decided your prediction.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A critical provider changes schema during an outage while retries duplicate effects, cached fallback misleads users, and no vendor-exit rehearsal exists. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules. A defensible solution applies Distributed Effect, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to controlled integration and resilience evidence.

content.code:
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: At-least-once delivery becomes exactly once with a deduplication set. This fails the Distributed Effect contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign effect identity, idempotency, ordering, concurrency, retry, timeout, unknown-outcome, compensation, and reconciliation rules.
